---
title: "Tools & Data"
description-meta: "Data sources and software tools used or produced by the MMLoS project"
---

# Tools & Data Resources

## Datasets Under Consideration

During the project we will use and evaluate the following datasets:

### National & Open Data
| Dataset | Provider | Coverage | Use |
|---------|----------|----------|-----|
| **OpenStreetMap (OSM)** | OpenStreetMap contributors | Global | Base network, infrastructure tags (cycleways, crossings, surfaces) |
| **OSM-RN (OS Multi-modal Routing Network)** | Ordnance Survey | England & Wales | Link geometry, average speeds, road widths |
| **STATS19** | DfT | England | Road traffic collisions (pedestrians, cyclists, horses) |
| **DfT Road Traffic Statistics** | DfT | England | Annual average daily flows, vehicle classifications |
| **National Travel Survey (NTS)** | DfT | England | Trip purposes, mode choice reasons, travel patterns |
| **Census / Census 2021** | ONS | England | Population, commuting flows, demographic data |
| **Bus Open Data Service (BODS)** | DfT | England | Stop/route timetables, actual running times, vehicle info |

### Project-Specific & Derived
| Dataset | Source | Status |
|---------|--------|--------|
| **Propensity to Cycle Tool (PCT) data** | ITS Leeds | Already available for Leeds case study |
| **LiDAR point clouds** | IFP-r (subcontract) | To be collected at 2 case-study sites (2026–2027) |
| **Case-study traffic counts** | Local authority partners | TBD — Essex, West Midlands, Leeds |

## Software Tools

### Existing Tools We Rely On

- **OSMnx** [@barron2014osmnx] — download and analyse OSM street networks in Python
- **QGIS** — GIS analysis and visualisation
- **R** (with `sf`, `tidytransit`, `osmdata`) — statistical analysis and network modelling
- **Pandana** — network accessibility analysis

### Tools in Development

| Tool | Status | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **MMLoS Python library** | Design phase | Core calculation engine for link and junction scores |
| **QGIS plugin** | Concept | Desktop tool for local authority users |
| **Web application** | Planned | Browser-based interactive tool (see [Approach](/approach.qmd)) |

### External Platforms Referenced

| Platform | URL | Note |
|----------|-----|------|
| **Plan.activetravelengland.gov.uk** | Browse & Sketch tools | ATE's design platform; we aim to integrate with or complement it |
| **Streetmix** | https://streetmix.net/ | Collaborative street design; useful for engagement |
| **3DStreet** | https://3dstreet.app/ | 3D visualisation of street cross-sections |

## Repository Structure

All project code will be archived under the [multilevelos GitHub organisation](https://github.com/multilevelos):

| Repository | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| `multilevelos/multilevelos.github.io` | This website |
| `multilevelos/bid` | Original research proposal and supporting documents |
| `multilevelos/tools` | Core MMLoS toolkit (Python, R, or both — TBD) |
| `multilevelos/analysis` | Statistical analysis notebooks and results |
| `multilevelos/data-schemas` | Data model definitions and validation rules |

More repositories will be added as the project progresses.

## Data Privacy & Ethics

- All public data used will be openly licensed (OSM, government open data)
- Any survey data collected will be anonymised and stored in compliance with UK GDPR
- The project has been designed with **data minimisation** principles: we will only collect data necessary for the MMLoS methodology
- LiDAR scans will be processed to remove personally identifiable information (faces, vehicle registration plates) before any data is shared

---

*For questions about data access or code use, please contact the [project team](/team.qmd).*
